content: Spring Batch gives chunked readers/writers, JobRepository, restart, skip/retry policies; but its value is chunked file/DB processing, not event-driven ingestion. Universal principles regardless of framework: processors must be stateless+idempotent (chunks retried, records seen twice); watermark = timestamp/cursor boundary between processed/unprocessed, extract with safety overlap window and rely on dedup to discard overlap; poison records handled via bounded retry then dead-letter status (skip policy or explicit FAILED state with error payload); restartability only works if writers designed idempotent from day one. For a modular monolith with staging tables, a custom job/job_run table plus idempotency key (source_system, external_id, content_hash) is the common lightweight pattern; Spring Batch justified only for large file-shaped backfills.
